title: Web Agents
summary: Show how a browser-based agent researches across web pages and documents, then generates a slide deck as the final output.
inputPlaceholder: Describe the topic the web agent should research and turn into a deck.
examplesCaption: Choose a prompt, then generate the deck to simulate web research, document review, and slide creation.
outputCaption: The final output is a static PDF deck shown after the research pipeline finishes.
pipelineSteps: Searching relevant web pages for coding-agent interpretability resources
Opening policy notes, technical blogs, and reference documents
Reading PDFs and extracting recurring themes
Organizing findings into slide sections and speaker-ready summaries

examplePrompts.label: Interpretability deck
examplePrompts.prompt: Research the error propagation in coding agents and their interpretability by reviewing web articles and research articles. Then generate a concise slide deck for teachers that explains the key issues and potential solutions
